Comment: Dr. Giancaspro is a fantastic professor, he gives great lectures and explains the material clearly. However, you need to stay consistently up to date with the coursework to do well. I personally fell behind, which led to poor test scores. Go to class and do as much practice as you can to succeed.
Tags: Tough grader, Amazing lectures, Test heavy

Comment: Teaches a tough class but on top of this is not lenient with grading and doesn't seem to offer much assistance. After talking with classmates in the other section, his version of the class is much more difficult and unnecessarily complicated.
Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things

Comment: You need to understand the first exam content or you will not pass. For me class was very anxiety inducing because I was so behind, but I was reviewing every day. The homework was unhelpful because if you can't do it on your own the mastering will not help.
Tags: Tough grader, Get ready to read, Graded by few things

Comment: Took him for both CAE 210 and 211. Great in both but can definitely tell he uses 210 as a weed out class. Neither were too difficult being very elementary engineering courses. As long as you attend lecture and do some practice problems you should have no issues getting a good grade.
Tags: Amazing lectures, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy

Comment: Fun lecturer, easy to meet outside of class, and loves engineering.  210 isn't that hard.  Go to every class and download a copy of the notes after every lecture.  You have to practice problems A LOT.  That helped me the most.  He sometimes picks exam questions from the homework, discussion, or supplementary list.  Do them and you'll do fine.
Tags: Lots of homework, Respected, Accessible outside class

Comment: Dr. G has been the most helpful professor to me since I started at UM.  I heard this class was tough but the way he goes over course material is truly helpful. He is always willing to help even on his own time.  I seriously would recommend him as a professor if you have a choice!
Tags: Amazing lectures, Respected, Accessible outside class

Comment: This teacher is beyond terrible. He takes it way too seriously and expects you to know Mechanics of Solids like you've been working in the field for 10 years, not like a student. I have never failed a class in my entire life and had a 3.8 GPA before I took him. I recommend him to absolutely no one unless you want to be stressed and ruin your GPA.
Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things

Comment: Fantastic lecturer and very willing to help. That being said, I had to drop out because I was failing and out of our class of about 35 people, about 75% failed/dropped the class. I still went to class though and learned a lot, but it was close to impassable for everyone. Cumulative grade was based on only 3 exams, 2 quizzes and 3% participation
Tags: Amazing lectures, Lots of homework, Graded by few things

Comment: Aced 520 last year. Definitely the best engineering prof and mentor at UM. 10/10 Recommend
Tags: Gives good feedback, Respected, Accessible outside class

Comment: Professor giancaspro is a great professor and very well organized in teaching methods, course outline, and handouts. He is very approachable and willing to help his students. It is definitely a class with tough material but he does a really good job in teaching it. This class definitely challenged me but I really enjoyed it nonetheless.
Tags: Accessible outside class, Skip class? You won't pass., Caring

Comment: I struggled with physics but this professor restored my hopes in engineering. He makes statics look so straightforward. He posts the lecture notes after class which is really useful if you miss something in class or you're sick. I wish he was teaching more courses in my major.
Tags: Respected, Skip class? You won't pass., Amazing lectures

Comment: every time before the test he would say to focus on particular topics or problems, but wont include it in the test. so be prepared that he would do smth unexpected, because i made a mistake listening to him, and then being completely shocked on the exam. totally passive aggressive, really subjective about partial credit
Tags: Skip class? You won't pass., Test heavy, Tough grader

Comment: Excellent professor, knows his stuff really well. His syllabus is super detailed and planned out to each day. Easy to reach outside of class and very responsive to emails. Overall great course and professor.
Tags: Respected, Accessible outside class, Inspirational

Comment: Dr. G is a GREAT professor. The class content itself is incredibly difficult so practice is EVERYTHING. He truly wants to help students in any way possible. I had him for CAE 210 and 211 and in both courses you must work hard for your grade spending hours doing homework, but it pays off during the exams. If you want to LEARN take him.
Tags: Lots of homework, Skip class? You won't pass., Clear grading criteria

Comment: Dr. G is an outstanding professor. The class is fast paced but he explains concepts really well and always checks if students understand it. He has lots of handouts and solves many sample problems. He recorded some of the solutions on video if you miss class. He's really helpful in office hours. I'll take him again if I have the chance.
Tags: Respected, Accessible outside class, Skip class? You won't pass.

Comment: The hws are extremely long. The exams are tough compared to the examples in class.
Tags: Lots of homework, Skip class? You won't pass., Test heavy

Comment: Optional homework! It's a double-edged sword because the exams are based heavily on hw. I was the only one to never miss a hw. I guarantee you this is why I did well on the tests, not some magic formula. If you're gonna do well in this class, at least care enough to do the hw. Or chegg your way through (pls dont, you'll end up with an empty brain).
Tags: TEST HEAVY, Amazing lectures, EXTRA CREDIT

Comment: Best professor in CAE department. DR. G, makes difficult topics easy. You do need to do all your homework and supplementary problems. It takes an infinite amount of time to do the homework but its crucial. You cant negotiate with him. Your HW due dates and exam dates are on syllabus as well as his test grading scheme.
Tags: Respected, LOTS OF HOMEWORK, Inspirational

Comment: This professor knows his material and he teaches it very well. he is not a tough grader, he is actually a fair one. his tests usually reflect the class notes and homework ( but not always) sometimes the tests are challenging. overall i would recommend him if you appreciate a learning environment.
Tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS, Amazing lectures, Would take again

Comment: Awesome professor but get ready to solve as many questions as possible
Tags: Get ready to read, Skip class? You won't pass., ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

Comment: A very well taught course with an extraordinary instructor.  Always available to meet with students and he does review sessions before the final exam.  A slightly tough grader but the exams were fair if you attended class and did the homework and supplementary problems.
Tags: Gives good feedback, Respected, Skip class? You won't pass.

Comment: Great professor. He teaches this subject incredibly well and you will absolutely learn a lot if you can stick through this class. It is important to spend a few extra hours each week doing the optional supplemental problems he gives, annoying in the short term but absolutely pays off test day. I 100% suggest taking Dr. G!

Comment: His teaching is great but his exams are hard, his grading sucks and he is tough to deal with.

Comment: He is a good teacher and makes things seem very simple during his lectures. But when the tests come around you feel like you are staring at completely new problems that are significantly harder than the ones done in class. I would avoid this professor if you wanna keep your gpa up.
Tags: Tough Grader, Skip class? You won't pass.

Comment: I would highly recommend taking Dr. G for this course. It's a hard course, however he will set you up for success for the rest of your CAE courses. The exams are extremely hard so make sure you know how to do all of the homework problems!

Comment: The class is extremely hard
Dr. Giancaspro is one of the most brilliant professors I ever had. He will make you interested in the subject, his lectures are clear and extremely important if you want to pass.

This guy is a decent teacher, but extremely difficult to deal with. He is not understanding to anything. For ex, if you are 1 minute late to a class that is 1 point off your FINAL grade. Simply Brilliant.

He is the best teacher I have ever had in my educational career. The subject matter itself is hard, so no the class is not easy. But his ability to teach makes it almost painless. Do you work, and you'll do well.
Excellent teaching. Super tough tests. Still the best there is for the basics in civil

This class shouldn't be that hard , but with him the class is very defficults .even though the homeworks are long and challanging ,not even one quistation came from the Homework. Go to class and you will learn everything! He always tries to help and does as much as he can to make sure you understand the material. He gives a list of Supplementry problems and you will do fine if you solve them before each test.
do not recommend this professor at all !!!

Giancaspro explains concepts clearly with examples in class.  Weekly homeworks are long and challenging, but they are necessary to understanding the concepts.  Many of his test questions come from his list of supplementary problems.  You can do well in the class-Just listen during class, do the homeworks, and understand the concepts.
Do the supplementary problems. Half of the exam quetions come from those. Go to class!
He explains OK. He helps out of class. But he gives only two exams and one final, while Young gives 3 exams and a final and you can drop one exam, including the final.

To simple of examples during class time, but explains concepts very well.  There was an issue with some test problems including techniques/ideas we learned but didn't cover that much.  Homework is long and hard, but not impossible, but if you study for the tests in advance b/c he gives you half the problems, you'll be fine.  Much better than Young
